that long before his time Greek horoscopy had been introduced into

None can doubt the sincerity and honesty of Varahamihira
inasmuch as he mentions the authorities, both ancient Indian and
foreign, for his statements. In the above verse quoted from the
Paficasiddhantikft he expressly states that the and TIH'W were
expounded by ancient sages who must be Garga, Para-
sara, KSsyapa, Devala etc. He also pays tribute to the scholarship
of the Yavanas (vide II. 14). From this we are not justified in
inferring that everything in astrology and astronomy here was
borrowed from the "Greeks. It is true that our author adds many
Greek terms such as Kriya, Tavuru and Jutuma to the names
prevalent in India.]
